DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF ELIZABETH RAMSEY'S MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY AND REQUESTS FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART AS REFLECTED IN THE TENTATIVE RULING AND THE CLERK'S .DOC (WORD) MINUTES. [SEE CLERK'S .DOC (WORD) MINUTES) FOR DETAILS.] THE COURT CONCLUDES THAT THE WILLIAMS DECLARATION AND THE PROOFS OF SERVICE ARE UNCONTROVERTED AND THAT DEFENDANTS' RESPONSES WERE TIMELY SERVED. THE COURT ORDERS RELIEF AS TO CERTAIN DISPUTED DISCOVERY RESPONSES TO THE EXTENT THAT DEFECTS OTHER THAN TIMELINESS WERE RAISED IN THE MOVING PAPERS. SANCTIONS ARE DENIED BECAUSE THE COURT FINDS DEFENDANTS ACTED WITH SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION AND WITHOUT ABUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT REQUESTED SANCTIONS SO THERE IS NO OCCASION TO DETERMINE WHETHER PLAINTIFF ACTED WITH SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION AND WITHOUT ABUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. THE MOVING AND OPPOSING PARTIES, EACH SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER. THE COURT SHALL REVIEW AND SIGN ONE OF THEM OR ISSUE ITS OWN. JUDGE PRO TEM: JAMES FLEMING; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; NOT REPORTED. (D302)

Plaintiff Elizabeth Ramsey Notice Of Motion To Compel Discovery And Requests For Monetary Sanctions (Part 2 Of 2)
Off Calendar Aug-21-2017 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion To Compel Discovery And Requests For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-21-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Larisa K. Raymundo of the law offices of Gilsleider McMahon Molinelli & Phan (925-446-3117) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party

No other appearance is made in this matter

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF ELIZABETH RAMSEY'S Motion To Compel Discovery And Requests For Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part as reflected in the tentative ruling and the clerk's .doc (word) minutes. [See Clerk's .doc (word) minutes) for details.] The Court concludes that the Williams declaration and the proofs of service are uncontroverted and that defendants' responses were timely served. The Court orders relief as to certain disputed discovery responses to the extent that defects other than timeliness were raised in the moving papers. Sanctions are denied because the Court finds defendants acted with substantial justification and without abuse of the discovery process. Defendants have not requested sanctions so there is no occasion to determine whether plaintiff acted with substantial justification and without abuse of the discovery process. The moving and opposing parties, each submit a [proposed] written order. The Court shall review and sign one of them or issue its own.

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)
